data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed amid Shifting Middle East Developments
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Wednesday evening, as investors navigate a rapidly evolving scenario dominated by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.11% and 0.09%, respectively, at 6:13 p.m. EST on March 4, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were down 0.03%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This follows a positive trading session earlier in the day. The rebound was driven by strength in big tech and semiconductor stocks. The Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq rose 0.5%, 0.8%, and 1.3%, respectively.

Investors now turn their attention to several major earnings reports due tomorrow. Kroger (KR), Burlington (BURL), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ) report Thursday morning, while Costco (COST) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) release results after the close. Weekly jobless claims are also due Thursday, adding another data point to a busy market week.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Should You Buy Costco Stock (COST) ahead of Q2 Earnings? Technical Indicators Suggest Yes
data.description:
U.S.-based warehouse chain Costco (COST) is slated to report fiscal Q2 earnings on March 5, after the market closes. Analysts project another strong quarter, with revenue expected to increase to $69.25 billion and EPS near $4.55. The company's momentum is supported by record holiday sales, strong traffic trends, and the ongoing strength of its membership‑fee model. Ahead of the results, COST stock is a Strong Buy, according to the technical indicators, implying further upside from current levels.

data.title: U.S.-Iran War Presents a 'Buying Opportunity,' Says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
The start of the U.S.-Iran war has reintroduced volatility into the stock market, although the underlying strength of the economy and healthy corporate earnings should reverse any short-term losses, according to strategists at Goldman Sachs.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We see correction risks as high given current valuations, but expect this to present a buying opportunity with relatively low risk of a more protracted and deep bear market," said Goldman Chief Global Equity Strategist Peter Oppenheimer.

U.S.-Iran War Poses Energy, Inflation Risks

Oppenheimer noted that market risks will correlate with the length of the war, as a longer conflict could disrupt the supply of energy and boost inflation. Iran borders the Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20% of global oil flows. Volume at the strait has plunged by 90% since the U.S. and Israel struck Iran over the weekend.

Furthermore, recent geopolitical shocks haven't resulted in a "long-lasting impact" on the market, said Oppenheimer. Separate data from Carson Investment Research shows that since World War II, the S&P 500 (SPX) has returned an average of 0.8% three months after a major geopolitical event and 3.0% six months after.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Micron (MU) vs. SanDisk (SNDK): Which AI Memory Chip Stock Has Greater Upside?
data.description:
Memory chip makers Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) are benefiting from a global shortage of AI memory, as demand for DRAM, NAND, and high-bandwidth memory continues to surge. With supply tight and prices rising, both stocks have rallied, but the key question for investors is which one offers greater upside from current levels. Using TipRanks' Stocks Comparison tool, we compare MU and SNDK to see which AI memory stock analysts favor now.

data.title: Top 3 Defense Stocks as the Iran War Continues
data.description:
Rising tensions in the Middle East are drawing attention to companies that build military equipment. Following the U.S.–Israeli strikes on Iran under Operation Epic Fury, investors are increasingly looking at defense contractors that produce missiles, aircraft, and advanced weapons systems. Historically, conflicts and geopolitical uncertainty often lead governments to increase military spending.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, companies that supply defense technology can see stronger demand and long-term contract growth. Below are three defense stocks that appear well-positioned to benefit if tensions continue to rise.

1. Lockheed Martin (LMT)

Lockheed Martin is the largest defense contractor for the U.S. government and plays a major role in producing advanced military systems. More specifically, the company manufactures the F-35 stealth fighter jet, which has become one of the most widely used aircraft among NATO allies. In addition, recent reports that Germany may purchase additional F-35 jets have helped boost investor interest in the stock.

From a business standpoint, the company ended 2025 with a massive $194 billion backlog, meaning it already has years of orders lined up. Impressively, that backlog equals roughly two and a half times its annual revenue, which reached $75 billion after growing by 6% last year. Much of that growth came from strong deliveries of F-35 jets and missile defense systems. Going forward, Lockheed is expected to increase production of PAC-3 MSE interceptor missiles, with output planned to jump from 600 missiles per year to around 2,000.

2. RTX (RTX)

Another major defense contractor that could benefit from rising military activity is RTX, the parent company of Raytheon. Indeed, the firm produces the Tomahawk cruise missiles that were reportedly used during the recent strikes on Iran. Because of this demand, Raytheon is now working with the Pentagon to increase production to as many as 1,000 Tomahawk missiles per year.

RTX has also been delivering strong financial results. In 2025, the company reported $88.6 billion in sales, while its order backlog reached a record $268 billion, up 23% from the previous year. Interestingly, nearly half of that backlog comes from international customers.

3. Kratos Defense (KTOS)

Separately, while Lockheed and RTX dominate the traditional defense market, Kratos Defense focuses on newer technologies such as military drones, rocket systems, and space hardware. Importantly, the company has recently attracted attention as investors anticipate an increase in demand for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and advanced weapon systems.

Looking ahead, the company plans to increase the production of its Valkyrie combat drone by targeting around 40 units per year by 2027. In addition, Kratos expects revenue from its hypersonic weapons business to double to about $400 million in 2026.

Which Defense Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three stocks mentioned above, analysts think that KTOS stock has the most room to run. In fact, KTOS' price target of $116.93 per share implies 31.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: "Quantum-Resilient Security": Penny Stock SEALSQ (LAES) Jumps on New Protection Stack Launch
data.description:
Penny stock SEALSQ (LAES) surged over 7% on Wednesday after the post-quantum computing solutions provider said it is ready to offer its Quantum‑Resilient Vertical Security Stack to protect the next generation of quantum computers and the infrastructure that supports them.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SEALSQ Reveals a Silicon-Level Security Stack

SEALSQ is rolling out a full security architecture built for quantum computing environments, combining four layers of protection into one system.

The stack includes hardware‑based Root of Trust, post‑quantum cryptography, secure semiconductor designs, and secure access tools for cloud‑based quantum computing. Together, these components protect devices, communications, chip-level electronics, and cloud services across the entire quantum ecosystem.

Also, these modules work together to form a quantum‑resilient security stack that protects everything from the silicon hardware to the cloud. Embedding security at the silicon level will allow developers to build systems that are secure from the start, avoiding the inefficiencies of adding security later.

What's Ahead for SEALSQ?

SEALSQ has been actively investing across the quantum technology landscape to strengthen its "Quantum Made in USA" strategy and expand its global footprint. Last month, the company invested in EeroQ. It is worth noting that EeroQ is developing a quantum‑chip architecture based on electrons on helium, and SEALSQ views it as a key partner in building secure quantum hardware.

The company's preliminary 2025 results show strong top‑line momentum but continued investment-driven losses. LAES reported $18 million in revenue, up 66% year over year, with $8 million generated in Q4 alone.

Looking ahead, the company projects 50%–100% year‑over‑year revenue growth for Q1 2026, supported by rising demand for quantum‑resilient security and the scaling of its post‑quantum product lineup.

Is LAES a Strong Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, LAES stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on two Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average SEALSQ stock price target of $7.50 implies 79% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: AVGO Earnings: Broadcom Stock Rises as Financial Results Top Wall Street Estimates
data.description:
Broadcom's (AVGO) stock is up after the chipmaker reported quarterly financial results that beat Wall Street estimates across the board.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The Silicon Valley-based semiconductor maker announced earnings per share (EPS) of $2.05, which topped the $2.03 consensus forecast of analysts. Revenue of $19.31 billion beat the $19.18 billion that had been forecast on Wall Street. The company's sales were up 29% from a year earlier.

Looking ahead, Broadcom said that it expects a 68% margin for its earnings in the current quarter. Management also said they anticipate $22 billion in revenue this quarter. Analysts had sales of $20.56 billion penciled in for the company.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: RGTI Earnings: Rigetti Computing Stock Drops after Revenue Miss
data.description:
Shares of Rigetti Computing (RGTI) fell in after-hours trading after the quantum computing company reported earnings for its fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2025. Earnings per share came in at -$0.03, which was in line with analysts' consensus estimate of -$0.03 per share.

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Recover Iran War Losses as Solid Economic Data Boosts Sentiment
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed with solid gains on Wednesday. In addition, both ETFs have clawed back their losses from earlier this week as investors digested positive labor market and services data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • ADP announced that private employers added 63,000 jobs in February, the most since July 2025 and topping the estimate of 50,000. 
  • The ISM Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) jumped to 56.1 in February, marking the highest level since July 2022 and above the estimate of 53.8.
  • President Trump officially nominated Kevin Warsh to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell.
  • Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that Trump will raise the global tariff rate to 15% from 10% "likely sometime this week."
  • Apple (AAPL) introduced the MacBook Neo, its most affordable laptop yet with a starting price tag of $599. On Tuesday, the tech giant announced new MacBook Pro and Air models equipped with M5 chips.
  • At the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said that the company's $30 billion investment in OpenAI will likely be its last.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said the firm is a buyer of NVDA ahead of its GTC 2026 event from March 16‑19.
  • Tesla (TSLA) closed higher after Bank of America upgraded TSLA stock to "buy" from "hold" with a price target of $460.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Consumer discretionary was the best-performing sector on Wednesday, driven by easing inflation concerns stemming from falling energy prices. In addition, the sector tends to benefit when the stock market rises, which creates wealth for consumers and encourages spending. Upbeat private employment and services data also contributed to the rise.

Several consumer discretionary stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Tesla (TSLA)
  • Amazon (AMZN)
  • Expedia (EXPE)
  • Booking Holdings (BKNG)
  • Airbnb (ABNB)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, energy was the worst-performing sector as oil prices stabilized for the first time this week. At the same time, it's still the best performing sector year-to-date with a return of 25%.

On Tuesday, Trump directed the Development Finance Corporation to provide political risk insurance and financial security guarantees for ships crossing through the Persian Gulf, easing fears of oil supply disruption in the Strait of Hormuz. He also weighed sending U.S. naval escorts for oil and gas tankers in the passage.

Notable energy stocks trading lower include:

  • ConocoPhillips (COP)
  • Halliburton (HAL)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: RBC Capital Lowers Oracle (ORCL) Stock Price Target Ahead of Q3 Earnings. Here's Why
data.description:
Enterprise software and cloud computing company Oracle (ORCL) is scheduled to announce its results for the third quarter of Fiscal 2026 on March 10. ORCL stock has declined 22% so far in 2026 amid concerns about AI disruption, the company's high debt, and exposure to OpenAI. Heading into Q3 FY26 earnings, RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria reiterated a Hold rating on Oracle and lowered his stock price target to $160 from $195, citing some concerns, including the company's ability to fund its Stargate commitments.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Oracle to report earnings per share (EPS) of $1.71, reflecting a 16.3% year-over-year growth. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to rise about 20% to $16.9 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
